ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

lc_check.lc_validation_reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number 'SEG-26D-466123' does not contain an LC-related field label or bank term designation.

locations_found: Invoice page 1: header/branding 'Swedish Match' (company logo/brand name)
Invoice page 1: Address block footer 'Swedish Match Industries AB, Västra Drottningvägen 15, P.O Box 84, 52230 Tidaholm, Sweden'
Invoice page 1: signing text 'Swedish Match Industries AB, Logistics & Customer Service'
Invoice page 2: Address block footer 'Swedish Match Industries AB, Västra Drottningvägen 15, P.O Box 84, 52230 Tidaholm, Sweden'

pairing_validation.reasoning: All three pairing criteria are met: (1) Certificate references invoice 632597 which matches invoice 000632597 (leading zeros normalization). (2) Consignor 'Swedish Match Industries AB' is the invoice issuer. (3) Consignee 'Peter & Company Limited' matches the invoice address 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED'.

consignor.certificate_value: Swedish Match Industries AB, Box 84, SE-522 22, TIDAHOLM, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: Swedish Match Industries AB, Västra Drottningvägen 15, P.O Box 84, 52230 Tidaholm, Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in all invoice files
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - 'Swedish Match Industries AB' found explicitly in invoice address block footer on pages 1 and 2, in signing text on page 1, and as brand name in header.
MATCH - Country 'Sweden' explicitly stated in invoice address block. Additionally, VAT number prefix 'SE' in 'SE556005025301' confirms Sweden.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur - consignor name found explicitly in invoice
Land verification via indirect identifiers - VAT prefix SE confirms Sweden

consignor.motivation: Consignor 'Swedish Match Industries AB' is the invoice issuer, explicitly stated in the invoice address footer on both pages and in the signing text. Country 'Sweden' is explicitly stated in the address block and confirmed by VAT prefix SE in 'SE556005025301'. The company name matches exactly after normalization. MATCH.
consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Swedish Match Industries AB' in Sweden. Invoice issuer: 'Swedish Match Industries AB' in Sweden (address footer). Exact name match. Country match via explicit address and VAT prefix. Rule 4.1.0 gives direct MATCH. No further sequential testing needed.

consignee.certificate_value: Peter & Company Limited, P O Box 84-85, CUL DE SAC, CASTRIES, Saint Lucia
consignee.invoice_value: PETER & COMPANY LIMITED, P O BOX 84-85, CUL DE SAC, CASTRIES, ST. LUCIA

comparison_steps.description: Identify primary billing field in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice has 'Invoice address' field containing 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED, P O BOX 84-85, CUL DE SAC, CASTRIES, ST. LUCIA'. This is the primary billing field.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate consignee name with invoice billing address name
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - 'Peter & Company Limited' matches 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED' after case normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Compare country: certificate 'Saint Lucia' vs invoice 'ST. LUCIA'
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - 'Saint Lucia' and 'ST. LUCIA' are the same country after normalization (full name vs abbreviation).

rules_applied.description: Priority order - Invoice address (billing field) used as primary
Country normalization - Saint Lucia / ST. LUCIA

consignee.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Peter & Company Limited' matches invoice address (billing field) 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED' exactly after case normalization. Address 'P O Box 84-85, CUL DE SAC, CASTRIES' matches. Country 'Saint Lucia' normalizes to match 'ST. LUCIA'. MATCH.
consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate: 'Peter & Company Limited, P O Box 84-85, CUL DE SAC, CASTRIES, Saint Lucia'. Invoice billing address: 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED, P O BOX 84-85, CUL DE SAC, CASTRIES, ST. LUCIA'. Name: exact match after case normalization. Address: exact match. Country: 'Saint Lucia' = 'ST. LUCIA' (standard abbreviation). The invoice also has a separate 'Consignee' field with 'DRY & PHARM WHOUSE / PETER & CO. DRY & PHARM' which is the delivery/warehouse party — this is secondary per 4.2.0.2. The primary billing field matches perfectly.

Safety Matches

goods_description.invoice_value: Safety Matches (Goods summary, page 2); Three Stars (item lines, page 1); Invoice no 000632597, date 2026-03-05

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers
comparison_steps.result: NOT_APPLICABLE - Certificate does not contain specific article numbers. Article numbers in invoice (0212215, etc.) are not referenced in certificate.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.3 Priority 2 - Text matching of identity-bearing designation
comparison_steps.description: Identify main product designation in certificate
comparison_steps.result: Certificate states 'SAFETY MATCHES'. Invoice Goods summary on page 2 states 'Safety Matches'. Exact match after case normalization.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 Priority 3 - Generic goods with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Certificate contains 'See attached invoice 632597 dated 260305 for item description'
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - Certificate references invoice 632597. Invoice number is 000632597 (leading zeros). Date '260305' interpreted as 26-03-05 (YYYY-MM-DD partial: 2026-03-05). Invoice date is 2026-03-05. Both reference and date match. The expression 'See attached invoice...for item description' constitutes an explicit invoice reference.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that invoice content is consistent with certificate description
comparison_steps.result: Certificate describes 'Safety Matches'. Invoice goods summary confirms 'Safety Matches'. Invoice item lines show 'Three Stars' which are a brand of safety matches. Content is consistent.

rules_applied.description: Identity-bearing designation 'SAFETY MATCHES' found as 'Safety Matches' in invoice goods summary
Generic goods with invoice reference - certificate references invoice 632597 dated 260305, matching invoice 000632597 dated 2026-03-05

goods_description.motivation: The certificate's identity-bearing product designation 'SAFETY MATCHES' is found exactly in the invoice's Goods summary as 'Safety Matches' (page 2). Additionally, the certificate contains an explicit invoice reference 'See attached invoice 632597 dated 260305 for item description'. The invoice number 000632597 matches 632597 (leading zeros removed). The date '260305' corresponds to 2026-03-05 (the invoice date). The invoice content (Safety Matches) is fully consistent with the certificate description. MATCH via both Priority 2 and Priority 3.
goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate box 6: 'SAFETY MATCHES / MADE IN SWEDEN / 996 cartons of Safety Matches / Safety Matches / See attached invoice 632597 dated 260305 / for item description'. Invoice page 2 Goods summary: 'Safety Matches'. Direct text match of identity-bearing designation. Invoice reference 632597 = 000632597 (leading zeros normalization allowed). Date 260305: interpreted as 26-03-05 i.e. 2026-03-05 matching invoice date. Expression 'See attached invoice...for item description' is an explicit invoice reference per 4.3.5.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 states '7324 kg gross'. This is the verification-grounding quantity per rule 4.4.2.1.
Search for numeric value 7324 in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Found on invoice page 2 in Goods summary: 'Average gross weight KG: 7 324'. After thousands separator normalization (space removed), value is 7324. Category is gross weight KG. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice value '7 324' with space as thousands separator normalized to '7324'
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - Both values are integers. Space is a valid thousands separator. Normalized value 7324 matches certificate value 7324.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate specifies 'kg gross'. Invoice specifies 'Average gross weight KG'.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - Both specify gross weight in KG. Weight category and unit match.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate also contains '996 cartons' in box 6. Invoice goods summary shows '996 CRT'. Checking for contradictions.
comparison_steps.result: No contradiction - 996 matches 996. Additionally verified as shipment quantity.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 value '7324 kg gross' used as verification-grounding quantity
Direct identification - value 7324 found in invoice gross weight
Weight category gross weight confirmed in both documents

quantity.motivation: Certificate box 7 states '7324 kg gross'. Invoice page 2 Goods summary states 'Average gross weight KG: 7 324'. After thousands separator normalization (space removed per 4.4.3.3), the numeric value 7324 matches exactly. Weight category is gross weight in both documents. Unit is KG in both documents. Additionally, the certificate states '996 cartons' in box 6 which matches '996 CRT' in the invoice goods summary — no contradiction. MATCH.
quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: '7324 kg gross'. Numeric value: 7324. Unit: KG. Category: gross. Invoice page 2 goods summary: 'Average gross weight KG: 7 324'. After removing space as thousands separator (4.4.3.3 — both values are integers, space is valid separator), normalized to 7324. Exact match. Invoice also shows 'Average net weight KG: 7 113' — this does not contradict certificate's gross weight. Certificate box 6 also mentions '996 cartons' — invoice goods summary shows '996 CRT' — consistent, no contradiction per 4.4.3.1.

evidence.section: Goods summary - Average gross weight KG

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Found: 'Country of origin: Sweden' on invoice page 1 bottom text. Also, 'Ctr' column shows 'SE' for all item lines.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'European Community; Sweden'. This is EU collection term + specific member state.
comparison_steps.result: Sweden is an EU member state. Invoice confirms 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Per 4.5.4.2, Sweden ↔ EU is valid.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'European Community; Sweden'. Invoice states 'Country of origin: Sweden'. This is EU collection term + one specific EU member state.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - Per 4.5.4.2.1, when certificate states EU + one specific member state, and invoice confirms EU origin (Sweden is EU member, and origin is explicitly Sweden), this is accepted as MATCH.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 - Check for non-EU origins in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Check if invoice contains any non-EU origin countries
comparison_steps.result: No non-EU origins found. All items show 'SE' in Ctr column and 'Country of origin: Sweden' applies to entire shipment.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit 'Country of origin: Sweden' field in invoice
EU normalization - European Community is EU collection term
EU member state ↔ EU equivalence - Sweden is EU member
EU + one specific member state accepted when invoice confirms origin as that member state

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate states 'European Community; Sweden' as origin. Invoice explicitly states 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Sweden is an EU-27 member state. Per rule 4.5.4.2.1, when the certificate states EU combined with one specific member state and the invoice confirms that member state's origin, this constitutes a MATCH. The invoice's 'Country of origin: Sweden' fully verifies both the 'European Community' (Sweden is EU member) and 'Sweden' components of the certificate's origin declaration. No non-EU origins found in the invoice. MATCH.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'European Community; Sweden'. This is a dual statement: EU collection term + specific member state Sweden. Invoice page 1: 'Country of origin: Sweden' (explicit origin field). Invoice item lines: 'Ctr' column shows 'SE' for all items. Applying 4.5.4.2.1: certificate states 'EU [Sweden]', invoice confirms 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Sweden is EU-27 member (post-Brexit). The invoice's explicit Swedish origin satisfies both the EU component (Sweden ∈ EU) and the Sweden component. No non-EU origins in invoice. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: No LC reference found in certificate
Document A is certificate (Certificate of Origin), Document B is invoice (2 pages). Single certificate, single invoice. Pairing is straightforward.
PASSED - All three criteria met: invoice reference matches, consignor identifiable, consignee identifiable.
MATCH - 'Swedish Match Industries AB' found as invoice issuer in address footer and signing text.
Sweden confirmed via explicit address and VAT prefix SE.
Invoice has 'Invoice address' field (primary billing field) with 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED'. Used as primary consignee reference.
MATCH - 'SAFETY MATCHES' found as 'Safety Matches' in invoice goods summary.
MATCH - Invoice reference 632597 matches 000632597. Date 260305 matches 2026-03-05. Expression 'See attached invoice...for item description' is explicit reference.
Box 7 value '7324 kg gross' is the verification-grounding quantity.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Direct identification of quantity in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH - 7324 found as '7 324' in invoice gross weight after thousands separator normalization.
Applied - space removed from '7 324' → '7324'. Both values are integers.
MATCH - Both specify gross weight in KG.
'Country of origin: Sweden' found explicitly on invoice page 1.
European Community is EU collection term. Sweden is EU-27 member.
MATCH - Certificate states 'European Community; Sweden', invoice confirms 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Sweden is EU member. Accepted per 4.5.4.2.1.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Invoice date mismatch with certificate date reference
rejected_hypotheses.reasoning: Certificate references invoice dated '260305'. This could be interpreted as 26-03-05 (2026-03-05) or as DD-MM-YY (26-03-05 = March 26, 2005). However, the invoice date is 2026-03-05, and the certificate issue date is 2026-03-03, making the interpretation 2026-03-05 the only logically consistent one. The format YYMMDD (260305 = 2026-03-05) matches the invoice date exactly. Rejected the alternative interpretation.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Consignee mismatch due to invoice Consignee field showing 'DRY & PHARM WHOUSE / PETER & CO. DRY & PHARM'
rejected_hypotheses.reasoning: The invoice has TWO consignee-relevant fields: (1) 'Invoice address' showing 'PETER & COMPANY LIMITED' and (2) 'Consignee' showing 'DRY & PHARM WHOUSE / PETER & CO. DRY & PHARM'. Per rule 4.2.0.2, the Invoice address (billing field) has priority. The certificate consignee 'Peter & Company Limited' matches the Invoice address exactly. The separate Consignee field appears to be a delivery/warehouse address. No MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantity discrepancy - certificate states '996 cartons' but invoice shows individual item quantities (200, 200, 239, 357 CRT)
rejected_hypotheses.reasoning: The invoice goods summary on page 2 shows total '996 CRT' which matches '996 cartons' in the certificate. Individual item line quantities sum to 200+200+239+357 = 996, consistent. No discrepancy exists.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Samtliga kontrollpunkter stämmer överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Avsändaren Swedish Match Industries AB, mottagaren Peter & Company Limited, varubeskrivningen Safety Matches, kvantiteten 7 324 kg brutto och ursprungslandet Sverige/EU kan alla verifieras i fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
non_blocking_observations: Certificate date reference '260305' interpreted as YYMMDD format matching invoice date 2026-03-05.
Invoice has separate 'Consignee' field (DRY & PHARM WHOUSE) distinct from 'Invoice address' (PETER & COMPANY LIMITED). Billing field used per priority rules.

sections.rule_applied: Identity-bearing designation 'Safety Matches' found in invoice goods summary. Invoice reference 632597 matches 000632597. Date 260305 matches 2026-03-05.
sections.motivation: Huvudbeteckningen 'Safety Matches' återfinns i fakturans varusammanställning. Certifikatets fakturahänvisning (632597 daterad 260305) matchar fakturanummer och datum. Innehållet är konsistent.

sections.rule_applied: EU + one specific member state (Sweden). Invoice confirms 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Sweden is EU-27 member. Accepted per EU normalization rules.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'European Community; Sweden'. Fakturan bekräftar 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Sverige är EU-medlem, vilket verifierar både EU-angivelsen och det specifika landet. Inga icke-EU-ursprung finns i fakturan.

human_readable_report.summary: Alla fem kontrollpunkter (avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet, ursprungsland) är verifierade som MATCH. Certifikat SEG-26D-466123 kan verifieras mot faktura 000632597. Rekommendation: automatiskt godkännande.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_details: All 5 applicable checkpoints have status MATCH. No MISMATCH or unresolved MANUAL_REVIEW. comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent. workflow_recommendation AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent. All checkpoint confidences ≥ 0.85. Overall confidence 0.97 ≥ 0.85 threshold.
